concepts.title: Finding Factorial of a number
concepts.conceptBody:
Factorial

In mathematics, the factorial of a non-negative integer $n$, denoted by $n$ !, is the product of all positive integers less than or equal to $\text{n}$.

For example:

$6 !=6*5*4*3*2*1

also $(\mathrm{n}+1) !=(\mathrm{n}+1) \quad \mathrm{n} !$

Special case: 

Zero factorial

This is a special case whose value is 1.

$\therefore \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }0!=1$

examples.title: Assuming that no two consecutive digits are same, the number of $n$ digit numbers, is
How many words can be formed from the letters of the word COURTESY, whose first letter is $C$ and the last letter is $Y$
How many words can be formed with the letters of the word MATHEMATICS by rearranging them
In how many ways can 5 boys and 5 girls stand in a row so that no two girls may be together 97]
